title: Scriptorium Development Ledger

statusLegend.yellow: Present or claimed, but unaudited / unverified. Yellow is audit debt.
statusLegend.red: Not started, failed audit, known bad, blocked, or intentionally absent.

integratedDeliverable: A scholarly workspace for texts, locators, annotations, citations, threads, search, and cited retrieval output.
workflowRule: Move sequentially. One active gate at a time. Do not advance until the active gate is implemented, audited, CI-verified, and closed.

gates.title: Foundation / repo / architecture
gates.rationale: Base repository, app, schema, roadmap, and CI foundation.
gates.completionStandard: Repository has product docs, app skeleton, Prisma model, roadmap, and CI foundation.
evidence: README.md, ROADMAP.md, prisma/schema.prisma, and CI foundation exist.

gates.title: PDF scholarly workflow
gates.rationale: First complete proof of source metadata, page mapping, highlights, notes, citations, persistence, and reload.
gates.completionStandard: PDF scholarly workflow passes fixture, typecheck, build, and migration checks.

gates.title: TXT + Markdown scholarly ingestion
gates.rationale: Plain text and Markdown validate non-PDF text snapshots and locator rules before Office formats.
gates.completionStandard: TXT and Markdown upload, annotation, citation, persistence, and reload pass CI.

gates.title: Snapshot policy for text formats
gates.rationale: Text formats need durable versioned snapshots so annotations survive reimport.
gates.completionStandard: Snapshots are normalized, checksum-addressed, versioned, and verified.

gates.title: DOCX ingestion path
gates.rationale: DOCX support follows stable text snapshots because Office extraction has more failure modes.
gates.completionStandard: DOCX extracts readable text, snapshots it, supports annotation/citation, and passes CI.

gates.title: CSL source editor
gates.rationale: Citation metadata needs controlled editing before broader exchange workflows.
gates.completionStandard: CSL-compatible source records validate and persist.

gates.title: Citation import/export
gates.rationale: Scholarly source data needs bounded standard-format exchange.
gates.completionStandard: CSL JSON, BibTeX, and BibLaTeX exchange paths pass fixtures without source corruption.

gates.title: Zotero-compatible data path
gates.rationale: Local Zotero-shaped data compatibility follows stable citation exchange.
gates.completionStandard: Zotero-shaped book item data round-trips through Source.cslJson.

gates.title: Research threads
gates.rationale: Annotations become scholarship when grouped into ordered research trails.
gates.completionStandard: Threads persist ordered annotations, citations, source references, notes, and tags.

gates.title: Thread export
gates.rationale: Stable threads should render usable writing artifacts.
gates.completionStandard: Threads render to Markdown and RTF with order, notes, locators, citations, and source context preserved.

gates.title: Exact search
gates.rationale: Search begins with exact, inspectable, source-linked results.
gates.completionStandard: Documents, extracted passages, notes, tags, citations, and threads search with source and locator links.

gates.title: Semantic search
gates.rationale: Similarity retrieval must rest on trusted extracted passages and exact source linkage.
gates.completionStandard: Similarity results return source-linked passage records with stable locators.

gates.title: Grounded answer generation
gates.rationale: The final layer must use trusted retrieval.
gates.completionStandard: Outputs cite recovered source passages only.
gates.note: Active final gate, but not started. It remains red until implementation exists and passes audit.

history.summary: Scriptorium was separated from Universal Office and defined as a scholarly reading platform.
Issue #1 and PR #2 closed the PDF scholarly workflow.
Issue #6 and PR #7 added TXT/Markdown ingestion.
Issue #8 and PR #9 added checksum-addressed snapshots.
Issue #10 and PR #11 added DOCX ingestion.
Issue #12 and PR #13 added CSL source editing.
Issue #14 and PR #15 added citation import/export.
Issue #16 and PR #17 added local Zotero-shaped compatibility.
Issue #18 and PR #19 added persistent research threads.
Issue #20 and PR #21 added Markdown and RTF thread rendering.
Issue #22 and PR #23 added bounded exact search with source and locator links.
Issue #24 and PR #25 added local deterministic source-linked similarity retrieval over trusted passages.
